title: BrewSpec v1.0
description: An open standard for describing coffee brews.

brews.description: Array of brew records. At least one brew is required.

date.description: Brew date. Accepts YYYY-MM-DD (date-only) or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (full UTC datetime).

dose_g.description: Coffee dose in grams. Must be > 0.

water_g.description: Recipe target water in grams. Must be > 0. Renamed from water_weight_g in v1.0. Context: brew-level fields represent recipe intent (target); result-level fields represent actual measurements.

yield_g.description: Recipe target output weight in grams. Must be > 0. Primarily useful for espresso dialling — the intended liquid yield before pulling the shot. For actual output weight measured after brewing, use result.yield_g.

brew_ratio.description: Water-to-coffee ratio expressed as a single float (grams of water per gram of coffee). e.g. 15.5 represents 15.5:1 or approximately 64g/L. Can be computed from water_g / dose_g. When both are present, tools should validate consistency; mismatches should be surfaced as a warning, not a schema error.

water_temp_c.description: Water temperature in celsius. Optional. Range 0-100 inclusive. Constrained to 0.1-degree precision (multipleOf: 0.1). New constraint in v0.8.

grind.description: Grind size. Standard vocabulary from finest to coarsest: turkish, espresso, fine, medium_fine, medium, medium_coarse, coarse.

duration_s.description: Brew duration in seconds. Must be > 0.

process_notes.description: Brew-process notes — operational observations about the preparation (e.g. 'washed filter paper', 'water from Brita filter', 'grinder re-calibrated'). For sensory description, use result.tasting_notes. Renamed from notes in v1.0.

coffee.description: Optional coffee ingredient descriptor. All fields optional.

name.description: A branded product name or human-readable descriptive label for the coffee (e.g. 'Ethiopia Yirgacheffe', 'Blue Bottle Hayes Valley Espresso', 'Estate'). Optional. Not required even when origins[] is populated. maxLength reduced from 150 to 100 in v1.0.

roaster.description: The company or person who roasted the coffee. Applies to the coffee as a whole, not to individual origin components.

roast_level.description: Roast level category. Deliberately coarse — three values cover the labels on the majority of retail bags. For finer roast detail, use process_notes. New in v0.8.

roast_date.description: Roast date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Plain date; no time component.

type.description: Whether the coffee is a single origin or a blend.

origins.description: Structured origin records. Array to support blends. Each entry is an origin object with all fields optional. minItems: 1 — omit the field entirely to record no origin data.

cupping_notes.description: Sensory notes on the coffee as a whole — typically from a bag description or a pre-brew cupping session (e.g. 'blueberry, jasmine, honey sweetness'). For a single-origin coffee, coffee.cupping_notes describes the coffee overall; origin.cupping_notes may duplicate it or be more specific. For blends, coffee.cupping_notes describes the blend as a whole while each origin.cupping_notes describes the individual component. New in v1.0.

origin.description: A single origin record within a coffee.origins array. All fields optional. Supports single-origin and blend records.

name.description: Descriptive name for this origin component. Plays the same role at the component level as coffee.name does at the coffee level. For single-origin coffees it will typically match coffee.name; for blends it is the name of this specific component (e.g. 'Brazil Natural', 'Colombia Washed').

region.description: State, province, or named growing region within the country.

subregion.description: District, zone, or sub-area within the region.

producer.description: Farm, estate, cooperative, or washing station name.

process.description: Green coffee processing method at origin. Distinct from the removed brew-level coffee.process field.

lot.description: Lot or batch identifier from the producer.

harvest_year.description: Year the crop was harvested. Four-digit integer.

varietal.description: Coffee varietal for this origin entry. Freeform. Records the coffee variety or cultivar specific to this component (e.g. Heirloom, Gesha, Bourbon). New in v0.6.

elevation_masl.description: Growing elevation in meters above sea level. Unit is embedded in the field name, following the established convention (dose_g, water_g, water_temp_c, duration_s, yield_g). New in v0.8.

cupping_notes.description: Per-component sensory notes for this origin entry (e.g. 'citrus and honey'). For single-origin coffees, this carries the cupping note for the whole coffee — same pattern as origin.name mirrors coffee.name. For blends, each component carries its own cupping notes. New in v1.0.

water.description: Optional water ingredient descriptor. All fields optional.

ppm.description: Water total dissolved solids in parts per million. Must be >= 0 if present.

equipment.description: Optional equipment descriptor. All fields optional.

grinder_setting.description: Grinder dial position or click setting used for this brew. Must be a positive number. Encoding convention: integer for primary increment grinders (e.g. 21 on a Comandante C40); decimal tenths for grinders with sub-steps between primary positions (e.g. 5.2 on a Fellow Ode Gen 2 means primary position 5, second sub-step). The schema does not enforce decimal precision — this convention is guidance for consistent encoding, not a constraint.

notes.description: Equipment state notes — observations about maintenance, calibration, filter type, or burr age. Distinct from brew-level process_notes, which record preparation observations.

pressure_bar.description: Line pressure or lever pressure in bars. Must be > 0. Primarily relevant to espresso — records pump pressure or lever force during extraction. New in v1.0.

flow_rate_ml_s.description: Volumetric flow rate in millilitres per second. Must be > 0. Useful for espresso profiling and controlled pour-over. New in v1.0.

result.description: Optional brew outcome descriptor. Groups measurements and sensory evaluation. All fields optional. Context: result-level fields represent actual measurements (as opposed to brew-level recipe intent).

tds.description: Total dissolved solids percentage of the finished brew. Must be > 0 if present.

ey.description: Extraction yield as a percentage (e.g., 20.1 for 20.1%). Must be > 0 if present. No maximum enforced.

brix.description: Dissolved sugar content in degrees Brix. A value of 0 is valid (distilled water). Must be >= 0 if present.

water_g.description: Actual water used in grams. Must be > 0 if present. Records the actual water weight used during the brew, which may deviate from the recipe target (brew.water_g). New in v1.0.

yield_g.description: Actual output weight of the brew in grams. For espresso, this is the liquid collected in the cup (distinct from brew.water_g, which is the input water target). For other brew types, yield_g may approximate water_g less absorbed water. Must be > 0 if present.

tasting_notes.description: Sensory description of the brew (e.g. 'Bright citrus acidity, caramel sweetness, clean finish'). For brew-process notes, use the brew-level process_notes field.

ratings.description: Optional multi-dimensional sensory ratings. Dimensions align with the SCA Coffee Value Assessment (CVA) affective protocol (SCA-104, 2024). All fields optional integers 1-9 (CVA hedonic scale: 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely).

overall.description: Holistic impression. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.

fragrance.description: Dry grounds aroma before water is added. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.

aroma.description: Wet aroma after water is added. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.

flavour.description: Taste and aroma experienced during drinking. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.

aftertaste.description: Length and quality of positive flavour attributes after swallowing. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.

acidity.description: Quality (not quantity) of acidity; brightness. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.

sweetness.description: Perceived sweetness. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.

mouthfeel.description: Tactile sensation; body and texture. 1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely.
